biteByNightBeginnerGuide.title: Bite By Night Beginner Guide
biteByNightBeginnerGuide.intro: Bite By Night is an asymmetrical Roblox horror game where one player becomes the Killer and the rest play as Survivors. Survivors choose a class, work through map objectives, power generators, and try to escape, while the Killer hunts them down before the round reaches 6 AM. In your first few matches, the biggest goal is not perfect play — it is learning how objectives, doors, and class abilities interact under pressure.

steps.title: Match Start and Role Assignment
steps.description: At the start of a round, one player is chosen as the Killer and everyone else becomes a Survivor. If you are the Killer, your job is to eliminate every survivor before the timer reaches 6 AM.

steps.title: Pick a Survivor Class
steps.description: Survivors choose a class before the round begins. The launch class lineup includes Customer, Fighter, Healer, and Security Guard, and each one changes how you help the team survive.

steps.title: Work Objectives and Generators
steps.description: Survivors must complete map objectives and power generators to open the exit. A teased objective called Electric Wiring has players hold the interaction key while fixing wires, and only one player can work an objective at a time.

steps.title: Buy Time with Doors, Terrain, and Utility
steps.description: You can slow the Killer by holding doors shut, barricading chokepoints, and using terrain smartly. Fighter can stun, Healer can recover health, Security Guard can reveal threats and generators, and Customer can self-sustain during chases.

steps.title: Understand the Win Condition
steps.description: Survivors win by finishing objectives, opening the exit, and escaping the map. Killers win by hunting down the full survivor team before they finish the escape route.

quickTips: Always have an escape route planned before starting an objective.
Use barricades at choke points to slow the Killer during chases.
Learn the map layouts early — knowing shortcuts saves your life.
Stick with at least one teammate for safety during objectives.

biteByNightReleaseDate.title: Bite By Night Release Date
biteByNightReleaseDate.intro: Release-date intent is one of the biggest search patterns for a new Roblox horror game, especially when early access footage, trailer drops, and Discord updates start appearing at the same time. Here is everything confirmed about the Bite By Night launch timeline.

cards.name: Official Public Release Date
cards.description: Multiple current Bite By Night launch references point to March 28, 2026 as the target public release date.

cards.name: Current Roblox Status
cards.description: The official Roblox experience page is currently tagged as EARLY ACCESS, signaling that launch rollout and access status are active topics for players to watch.

cards.name: Best Places to Track Updates
cards.description: The official Discord invite, trailer page, and Roblox listing are the fastest places to monitor release timing, access changes, and launch announcements.

cards.name: Launch Gameplay Snapshot
cards.description: Players enter rounds as either a Killer or a Survivor, then race objectives and escape routes against the Killer before the night timer ends.

biteByNightCodes.title: Bite By Night Codes
biteByNightCodes.intro: Code demand is extremely high around a Roblox game launch. For Bite By Night, the public code situation right now is straightforward: there are no active codes listed, no expired codes archived yet, and no confirmed public redemption flow shown in current guide coverage.

items.name: Current Status
items.description: Current code trackers list zero active Bite By Night codes. Codes are expected to drop with events and milestones after launch.

items.name: How to Redeem
items.description: Open the game on Roblox, look for the codes button in the main menu, enter the code exactly as shown, and claim your reward.

items.name: Where to Find Codes
items.description: Official codes are typically announced on Discord, X (Twitter), and the Roblox game description. Community sources like Reddit also track them.

items.name: Expired Codes
items.description: No expired codes yet. As codes are released and expire, they will be tracked here for reference.

biteByNightClassesGuide.title: Bite By Night Classes Guide
biteByNightClassesGuide.intro: Bite By Night gives survivors a class choice before each round, and those class kits decide whether you play for self-preservation, peel, healing, or map control. The current launch class pool is Customer, Fighter, Healer, and Security Guard.

solutions.name: Customer
solutions.description: Pizza heals 20 percent of current HP. Drink grants a temporary speed boost and applies a slowness debuff after it ends. Best for solo survival and learning chase routes.

solutions.name: Fighter
solutions.description: Swing lunges with a fire axe and stuns the Killer. Parry blocks close attacks and adds charge for stronger follow-up pressure. Best for saving teammates and aggressive peel.

solutions.name: Healer
solutions.description: Heal restores teammate HP over time. Self Heal restores your own HP and helps stabilize between chases. Best for team-oriented groups and longer rounds.

solutions.name: Security Guard
solutions.description: Taser charges into a stronger stun or slowdown. Cams lets you watch map cameras and reveal animatronics or generators to teammates. Best for coordinated teams and objective support.

managementTips: Customer is free and great for learning the game basics.
Fighter is the go-to for aggressive plays and rescues.
Healer keeps the team healthy during extended rounds.
Security Guard provides the best map awareness and callouts.

biteByNightClassTierList.title: Bite By Night Class Tier List
biteByNightClassTierList.intro: Bite By Night currently has four known Survivor classes: Customer, Fighter, Healer, and Security Guard. Security Guard stands out as the most complete all-around pick thanks to cameras and stun utility, while Healer is the best pure support choice. Fighter is strongest in coordinated lobbies, and Customer remains the easiest free entry class for self-sustain and escape-focused play.

tiers.summary: The most balanced class in the current roster. It can scout the killer and objectives with cameras while also creating space with a charged taser stun.
The strongest dedicated support class. It keeps teammates alive, extends generator pressure, and is especially valuable when a team wants steady rounds instead of risky duels.
A stun-focused class with real team value, but it needs timing and coordination. Fighter is much better in groups that can capitalize on parries and axe stuns.
The default beginner class. It offers strong self-heal and escape tools for solo survival, but it contributes less team pressure than the more specialized classes.

biteByNightKillersGuide.title: Bite By Night Killers Guide
biteByNightKillersGuide.intro: The current known killer pool is split between the confirmed release roster and killers already listed as upcoming. Springtrap, The Mimic, and Ennard make up the confirmed launch lineup, while Marionette and Mangle are already marked for future addition. This module gives players a quick roster read before they dive into matchup-specific guides and tier lists.

killers.name: Springtrap
killers.summary: Springtrap is the most rounded killer in the current pool, combining offense, crowd control, and reliable chase pressure.

killers.name: The Mimic
killers.summary: The Mimic trades raw consistency for flexibility. It can swap between different modes to adapt to the situation, but it needs cleaner decision-making than the other killers.

killers.name: Ennard
killers.summary: Ennard is built around deception and surprise. It rewards players who can use disguise pressure and catch grouped Survivors before they react.

killers.name: Marionette
killers.summary: Marionette is already listed as an upcoming killer, making it one of the next major roster expansions to watch.

killers.name: Mangle
killers.summary: Mangle is also listed in the upcoming section, giving the game another confirmed killer planned beyond the release pool.

biteByNightKillerTierList.title: Bite By Night Killer Tier List
biteByNightKillerTierList.intro: Among the currently ranked killers, Springtrap has the strongest overall toolkit thanks to its combination of tracking, traps, mobility, and finishing power. Ennard follows closely with strong disguise-based ambush play, while The Mimic offers creative flexibility but needs more setup and cleaner execution to match the others. Upcoming killers are excluded until their kits are more fully established.

tiers.summary: The best all-around killer right now. Springtrap brings trap denial, long-range finishing pressure, tracking utility, and strong mobility in one package.

Ennard is excellent when disguise play lands. It can punish grouped Survivors and create sudden momentum swings, but it is slightly less reliable than Springtrap once experienced players start reading it.
The Mimic has useful flexibility through Speed, Strength, and Stealth modes, but none of those options are as naturally reliable as Springtrap or Ennard when lobbies get stronger.

biteByNightSurvivorGuide.title: Bite By Night Survivor Guide
biteByNightSurvivorGuide.intro: As a Survivor, your job is to work with the team, complete puzzles and minigames, power the generators, and open the exit before the killer wipes the lobby. Class choice changes how you contribute: Customer survives on self-sustain, Fighter creates stun windows, Healer keeps the team alive, and Security Guard scouts danger and sets up safer routes. Good Survivor play is less about panic running and more about timing, positioning, and using each class for the right moment.

steps.title: Choose a class with a clear job
steps.description: Customer is the free starter class for self-heal and escape. Fighter is your stun specialist, Healer is your sustain support, and Security Guard is the vision-and-control pick with cameras and a taser.

steps.title: Prioritize generators and map tasks early
steps.description: Survivors win by completing puzzles and minigames that power generators and open the exit. Early match time is best spent pushing objectives before the killer builds too much pressure.

steps.title: Use your abilities for space, not panic
steps.description: Customer should save Pizza and Drink for clutch survival, Fighter should look for deliberate stun windows, Healer should keep teammates active, and Security Guard should feed information with cams before committing the taser.

steps.title: Play around teammates instead of splitting without purpose
steps.description: The role is built around teamwork. Healer and Fighter become much stronger together, and Security Guard gets more value when the team actually uses the information from cameras and follow-up stuns.

steps.title: Turn the last objective into a clean escape
steps.description: Once generators are powered, stop taking unnecessary fights. Let utility classes create a safe window, keep healing if possible, and rotate straight toward the exit instead of giving the killer a second chase.

biteByNightSpringtrapGuide.title: Bite By Night Springtrap Guide
biteByNightSpringtrapGuide.subtitle: Springtrap is the release starter killer and the easiest launch character to understand at a glance. His kit mixes direct damage, route denial, and reveal pressure.
biteByNightSpringtrapGuide.intro: Springtrap is positioned as Bite By Night's launch anchor and expected first killer unlock. He pressures survivors by alternating between basic hits, axe control, trap placement, and a reveal sequence that can turn information into a fast follow-up chase.

sections.name: Launch Role
sections.description: Springtrap is a release killer and is expected to be the first killer unlocked by players. He is framed as the starter killer for the game.

sections.name: Base Damage Profile
sections.description: Swing is Springtrap's LMB attack. With the axe equipped, the attack deals 36 damage. Without the axe, his fist deals 28 damage.

sections.name: Remnant Cleaver
sections.description: Remnant Cleaver is used on Q. Springtrap slows down, throws his axe, and a hit applies Weakness. After the throw, he must return to the axe and hold E to retrieve it.

sections.name: Trap and Reveal Sequence
sections.description: With the axe in hand, Springtrap can place a Beartrap on E. If a survivor steps on it, they take damage and are stunned for 10 seconds. After using Remnant Cleaver, that third-slot action changes into Scream, which highlights all survivors, and then into Charge for a fast grab follow-up.

sections.name: Pressure Pattern
sections.description: Springtrap's strongest flow is to control a route with Beartrap, force movement with the axe threat, then convert information from Scream into a commit with Charge. He is at his best when survivors are funneled through doors and narrow paths.

sections.name: Best Punish Windows
sections.description: Springtrap is easiest to contest while planting a Beartrap, during the slowdown before the axe throw, and after a failed Charge attempt. If he has already thrown the axe, forcing distance before he reclaims it cuts down his immediate close-range threat.

growthMilestones: Use Beartrap placement windows to create distance
Bait and dodge the Remnant Cleaver

biteByNightTheMimicGuide.title: Bite By Night The Mimic Guide
biteByNightTheMimicGuide.subtitle: The Mimic is a release killer built around stance swapping. Its mode system gives it different chase speeds, damage values, and ambush tools.
biteByNightTheMimicGuide.intro: The Mimic is one of the three confirmed release killers and has the most explicit stance-based kit shown so far. It can shift between Strength, Speed, and Stealth to trade raw damage for mobility, ambush pressure, or grab utility.

priorities.name: Launch Role
priorities.description: The Mimic is a confirmed release killer. It begins the round with Strength selected and uses Mode Switch on Q to access the rest of its kit.

priorities.name: Mode Damage Values
priorities.description: The Mimic's base Swing changes by mode. Strength mode deals 42 damage, Speed mode deals 10 damage, and Stealth mode deals 34 damage.

priorities.name: Speed Mode
priorities.description: Speed mode increases movement speed, removes the endlag for missing Swing, and unlocks Ground Slam. Ground Slam launches The Mimic forward, ragdolls a nearby survivor, and deals damage on impact.

priorities.name: Stealth Mode
priorities.description: Stealth mode unlocks Grab and Tunneling. It gives The Mimic the Undetectable status effect, mutes its footsteps, and lowers its basic hit damage to 34.

priorities.name: Grab and Tunneling Details
priorities.description: Grab can hold a survivor for about 7 seconds and follow up with an 18-damage punch before throwing the target away. Tunneling takes about 3 seconds to enter, grants speed and invisibility underground, and can erupt out of the floor to knock down nearby survivors while dealing 21 damage on emergence.

priorities.name: How to React to The Mimic
priorities.description: The safest way to read The Mimic is to track its current mode first. Strength means high hit damage, Speed means chase commitment, and Stealth means ambush pressure. Spacing matters because a thrown survivor can ragdoll teammates, and tunneling still creates loud digging noise even while underground.

biteByNightEnnardGuide.title: Bite By Night Ennard Guide
biteByNightEnnardGuide.subtitle: Ennard is a confirmed release killer centered on deception. Its toolkit is built to confuse survivor reads, pull targets into range, and punish teams that group carelessly around bodies.
biteByNightEnnardGuide.intro: Among the launch killers, Ennard leans hardest into trickery. Instead of pure route control or stance swapping, it threatens survivors with corpse disguise, wire-based pull tools, and delayed pressure that can turn a picked-off teammate into a second trap.

achievements.title: Launch Role
achievements.description: Ennard is a confirmed release killer and was the final killer finished for the launch roster. It joins Springtrap and The Mimic as the three killers listed for release.

achievements.title: Hijacking
achievements.description: Hijacking lets Ennard enter a deceased player and wear them as a disguise. While disguised, movement is more sluggish, and survivors cannot hear Ennard's chase theme.

achievements.title: Disguise Burst Threat
achievements.description: When ready to attack, Ennard can burst out of the corpse disguise, sending body pieces around the area and damaging nearby players on contact. While disguised, it can also land a stabbing attack with an exposed wire.

achievements.title: Pull Tool
achievements.description: Pull throws an extended wire in a straight line. If it connects, the survivor is dragged toward Ennard until they are inside basic attack range.

achievements.title: Wirething Pressure
achievements.description: Wirething releases a tracking device from Ennard's knee. It follows survivors and is meant to explode on contact, and players must shake their screen to remove it before the bar fills.

achievements.title: How to Play Around Ennard
achievements.description: Springtrap controls space and The Mimic changes tempo by mode, but Ennard creates the most mindgame pressure around corpses and disguise checks. Survivors should confirm bodies before grouping, avoid blind rescues around a fallen teammate, and respect any straight lane where Pull can drag them into melee range.

biteByNightMapsGuide.title: Bite By Night Maps Guide
biteByNightMapsGuide.subtitle: The official map pool currently names two confirmed maps: The Forest and Warehouse. Shared round systems matter just as much as scenery because doors, objectives, and timing define how both sides position.
biteByNightMapsGuide.intro: Bite By Night's current official map list is compact, so the most useful guide structure is to pair each confirmed map with the core mechanics that shape every round. Survivors work one objective at a time, any class can barricade doors, and killers win by wiping the team before 6 AM.

maps.name: The Forest
maps.identity: The Forest is one of the two maps listed on the official Maps page and has also been described in a teaser as a map based on the FNAF 4 forest.
maps.sharedSystems: Electric Wiring is the only teased objective so far, and only one survivor can work an objective at a time.
maps.survivorTips: Use doors as stall points, rotate after objective progress, and avoid stacking the whole team into one approach lane.
maps.killerTips: Pressure isolated objective players, force short chases, and attack door control before survivors settle into safe space.

maps.name: Warehouse
maps.identity: Warehouse is the other named map on the official Maps page and gives the release pool a second confirmed setting alongside The Forest.
maps.sharedSystems: Barricading remains important here because any class can press F near a door to start a barricade minigame.
maps.survivorTips: Play around rooms and reset points, keep spacing between teammates, and do not let one failed door hold collapse the whole side of the map.
maps.killerTips: Lean into room-to-room pressure, challenge barricades quickly, and punish survivors who overcommit to one room or one objective.

maps.name: Shared Round Rules
maps.identity: Killers are trying to hunt down every survivor before the timer reaches 6 AM. A failed barricade minigame can end with the survivor getting knocked to the ground.
maps.sharedSystems: Doors, timed rounds, and single-user objective interaction are the main map-shaping systems currently shown on the official wiki.
maps.survivorTips: Split objective pressure, keep at least one escape path open, and save stun tools for doorway commits or recovery windows after killer abilities.
maps.killerTips: Shorten survivor rotations, force one-on-one checks at doors, and capitalize whenever only one survivor is locked into an objective animation.

biteByNightSkinsGuide.title: Bite By Night Skins Guide
biteByNightSkinsGuide.intro: Skins in Bite By Night range from common cosmetics to rare event-exclusive looks. The cosmetic system covers both survivor and killer appearances with various unlock methods.

faqs.question: How do skins work in Bite By Night?
faqs.answer: Skins change the visual appearance of your character without affecting gameplay. They are available for both survivors and killers, offering unique character designs.

faqs.question: How do I unlock Bite By Night skins?
faqs.answer: Skins can be earned through gameplay progression, in-game currency, special events, and code redemptions. Different skins have different unlock requirements.

faqs.question: Are there limited-time skins in Bite By Night?
faqs.answer: Yes. Seasonal events and special promotions offer exclusive skins that become unavailable after the event period ends. Check the official channels for upcoming events.

faqs.question: What rarity tiers exist for Bite By Night skins?
faqs.answer: Skins are organized by rarity from common to legendary. Higher rarity skins have more detailed designs and unique visual effects that stand out in matches.

faqs.question: Can I preview Bite By Night skins before unlocking?
faqs.answer: The in-game store and collection menu allow you to preview skins before spending currency or completing unlock requirements.

biteByNightMarionetteGuide.title: Bite By Night Marionette Guide
biteByNightMarionetteGuide.intro: Marionette is an upcoming killer teased for post-launch content in Bite By Night. Drawing from deep FNAF lore, Marionette is one of the most anticipated roster additions in the community.

settings.name: Current Status
settings.description: Marionette has been teased through developer hints and community discussion but is not yet playable. Watch official channels for reveal dates.

settings.name: FNAF Lore Connection
settings.description: The Marionette is one of the most iconic FNAF characters. Its Bite By Night version is expected to reimagine the character for asymmetrical gameplay.

settings.name: Expected Playstyle
settings.description: Based on community discussion, Marionette may feature puppet-like movement mechanics and unique zone control abilities that differ from launch killers.

settings.name: Community Anticipation
settings.description: Marionette is among the most requested killers in the Bite By Night community Discord and Reddit. Post-launch roster reveals are highly anticipated.

biteByNightLoreGuide.title: Bite By Night Lore Guide
biteByNightLoreGuide.intro: Bite By Night builds its own horror universe within Roblox, drawing heavy inspiration from Five Nights at Freddy's while creating original narrative elements. The lore is being revealed through trailers, teasers, and in-game details.

entries.title: FNAF Inspiration and Original Lore
entries.description: Bite By Night features animatronic killers inspired by FNAF but tells its own story. Character designs reference classic animatronics with unique Roblox interpretations.

entries.title: Killer Backstories
entries.description: Each killer has a backstory that connects to the game world. Springtrap, The Mimic, and Ennard all carry lore elements that players can discover through gameplay.

entries.title: Map Lore and Environmental Storytelling
entries.description: Maps contain environmental details that hint at the game's larger narrative. Facility rooms, forest structures, and industrial areas all carry story significance.

entries.title: Community Theories and Developer Reveals
entries.description: The community actively pieces together lore through trailer analysis, in-game clues, and developer Q&A sessions. New reveals are expected with each content update.

biteByNightCommunityFAQ.title: Bite By Night Community FAQ
biteByNightCommunityFAQ.intro: Answers to the most common questions players have about Bite By Night, from platform availability to gameplay mechanics and community resources.

steps.title: What platform is Bite By Night on?
steps.description: Bite By Night is a Roblox game available on all platforms that support Roblox, including PC, mobile, and console.

steps.title: Is Bite By Night free to play?
steps.description: Yes. Bite By Night is free to play on Roblox. Some cosmetic items and class unlocks may require in-game currency.

steps.title: How many players per match in Bite By Night?
steps.description: Bite By Night features asymmetrical gameplay with multiple survivors versus one killer per match.

steps.title: Is Bite By Night related to FNAF?
steps.description: Bite By Night is inspired by Five Nights at Freddy's and Dead by Daylight but is an original Roblox game, not an official FNAF product.

steps.title: Are there active codes for Bite By Night?
steps.description: Currently there are no confirmed active codes. Codes are expected to be released with future updates and events.

steps.title: When was Bite By Night released?
steps.description: Bite By Night launched on Roblox on March 28, 2026.

steps.title: Who made Bite By Night?
steps.description: Bite By Night was developed by OzelBlox and the Totally Not Secret Games team on Roblox.

steps.title: How do I report bugs in Bite By Night?
steps.description: Join the official Discord server to report bugs in the dedicated bug-reports channel.

faq.title: Frequently Asked
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Bite By Night

questions.question: What is Bite By Night?
questions.answer: Bite By Night is a Roblox asymmetrical horror game inspired by FNAF and Dead by Daylight. Survivors work together to complete objectives while a killer hunts them down.

questions.question: Is Bite By Night free?
questions.answer: Yes. Bite By Night is free to play on the Roblox platform.

questions.question: How many classes are in Bite By Night?
questions.answer: Bite By Night launched with 4 survivor classes: Customer, Fighter, Healer, and Security Guard, each with unique abilities and roles.

questions.question: How many killers are in Bite By Night?
questions.answer: Bite By Night launched with 3 killers: Springtrap, The Mimic, and Ennard, each featuring distinct abilities and playstyles.

questions.question: Are there codes for Bite By Night?
questions.answer: Currently there are no active codes for Bite By Night. Codes are expected to arrive with future updates and events.

questions.question: Where can I find the Bite By Night community?
questions.answer: The official Discord server has over 231,000 members. You can also follow @bitebynightdev on X and Instagram for the latest updates.

cta.title: Ready to Play Bite By Night?
cta.description: Join the Bite By Night community on Discord for guides, updates, codes, and discussions!
